Requirement Description: PA can edit supplier only when onboarding status is one of: consent pending, consent rejected, KYC approved, verification rejected.
Acceptance Criteria: Edit action is enabled only for the 4 listed statuses; Edit action disabled for all other statuses (e.g., verification pending, onboarding not started, etc.) and details can be edited in the manual verification pending status.
Dependencies/Notes: Source: PRD "Edit Supplier Information".

Requirement Description: GST edit flows supported for: (a) two active (one existing), one cancelled; (b) one active (existing).
Acceptance Criteria: System validates GSTIN lifecycle and allows edits only for supported combinations; Proper prompts for unsupported GST states (e.g., no active GST).
Dependencies/Notes: Align with GST verification service rules.

Requirement Description: User cannot change GST to another state; new supplier must be created.
Acceptance Criteria: Attempts to switch state code in GSTIN are blocked with guidance to create a new supplier; Existing supplier retains state code.
Dependencies/Notes: GSTIN state code equals first two digits; validate immutability.

Requirement Description: Bank edit: if status is approved, show screen with old and new bank details.
Acceptance Criteria: Edit screen shows previous bank details alongside new entry fields and change summary; Save requires explicit confirmation.
Dependencies/Notes: Requires secure masking for sensitive fields.

Requirement Description: Bank edit: if status is KYC manual approval pending → move back to consent pending.
Acceptance Criteria: On submit of bank edit while current status is "KYC manual approval pending", system transitions to "consent pending" and triggers consent flow.
Dependencies/Notes: Status transition and consent trigger integration.

Requirement Description: Bank edit: if status is consent pending and bank account edited, only new bank details shown.
Acceptance Criteria: UI shows only new bank details (no old vs new screen) when status is already consent pending.
Dependencies/Notes: Keep change history available elsewhere.

Requirement Description: All edits visible in app with earlier details (x → y).
Acceptance Criteria: Change history panel shows before/after values per field with timestamp, actor(optional), and reason (optional).
Dependencies/Notes: Audit log retention policy.

Requirement Description: If supplier onboarded and PA wants to update bank account, and a payment has been initiated: block update and notify PA to get it rejected first.
Acceptance Criteria: Attempt to edit bank when there is any payment in initiated state results in a block and instructional notification; No data is saved.
Dependencies/Notes: Integrate with payments service to detect initiated payments.

Requirement Description: If no payment initiated, on bank edit: block payment creation/initiation until supplier is approved again.
Acceptance Criteria: After bank edit submission, the system prevents payment creation/initiation until status returns to an approved state; Appropriate banner/tooltips shown.
Dependencies/Notes: Payments gating flag toggled on edit; cleared on approval.

Requirement Description: GST changes affect only new POs; existing POs retain old GST.
Acceptance Criteria: Existing POs keep prior GST values; New POs use updated GST from the time of change.
Dependencies/Notes: PO service must read GST snapshot at creation.

Requirement Description: Bank updates apply immediately; payments are blocked during the edit open period.
Acceptance Criteria: Bank details effective immediately after edit; Payments remain blocked until approval completes, then unblock.
Dependencies/Notes: Coordinate with KYC approval workflow.

Requirement Description: MSME change has three impacts on the flow: 1) MSME address selection affects only new POs, 2) MSME registered name influences auto-approval algorithm decisions.
Acceptance Criteria: Selecting MSME address affects only new POs; Existing POs remain unchanged Business name changes from MSME updates affect invoice generation (clarify existing vs new invoice impact) Auto-approval algorithm considers MSME registered name for approval decisions All impacts are properly tracked and audited
Dependencies/Notes: MSME address link to PO header defaults Invoice generation system integration for business name changes Auto-approval algorithm integration for MSME name consideration

Requirement Description: After KYC Approved, PA can add MSME and GST.
Acceptance Criteria: Add buttons for MSME and GST are visible and enabled only in KYC Approved status; Triggers validation and consent flows as applicable.
Dependencies/Notes: Align with consent rules.

Requirement Description: Consent message required for all edits/additions except GST Address edit.
Acceptance Criteria: Consent required and captured for all relevant changes; GST Address-only edits skip consent.
Dependencies/Notes: Consent template catalog, audit proof.

Requirement Description: Consent reminder messages throttled to maximum 5 per 24 hours.
Acceptance Criteria: System enforces a limit of 5 reminders per supplier per rolling 24h window; Excess attempts are suppressed with audit log.
Dependencies/Notes: Idempotency and rate limit storage.

Requirement Description: FYI message scope TBD.
Acceptance Criteria: Defined recipients/events documented and implemented after product sign-off.
Dependencies/Notes: PRD incomplete; requires clarification.

Requirement Description: For both edit and addition flows: highlight changed/added fields.
Acceptance Criteria: UI highlights changed fields and shows before/after summary at review step; Accessible color contrast meets guidelines.
Dependencies/Notes: Align with design system.

Requirement Description: For Individual PAN + Aadhaar + MSME + No GST + Bank details, require supplier license upload to link proprietor and business name.
Acceptance Criteria: Submission blocked until license document provided for the specified combination; Document linked in audit trail.
Dependencies/Notes: Doc type whitelist and OCR (if applicable).

Requirement Description: Improve GST Address details readability from JSON response and UI/frontend display.
Acceptance Criteria: GST address JSON response is properly structured and readable UI renders GST address clearly with structure, pin code, state code, and labels Support multiple addresses if present in JSON response JSON parsing and display handles all GST address formats correctly
Dependencies/Notes: Backend JSON response structure optimization Frontend JSON parsing and display logic UX review for both JSON structure and UI presentation

Requirement Description: Edge case: Supplier onboarded with Aadhaar only, later obtains PAN and GST — flow will be blocked.
Acceptance Criteria: System blocks the flow when business PAN is added to Aadhaar-only supplier Clear error message displayed to user explaining the blocking No further processing allowed for this combination User is guided to create new supplier instead
Dependencies/Notes: Define specific blocking mechanism (error message, redirect, etc.)
column6: need clarity Vidhatra Shukla on this currently updating this here

Requirement Description: Enforce 60s cooldown between consent reminders.
Acceptance Criteria: Attempt before 60s blocked with cooldown message (e.g., at 30s, 59s); At exactly 60s or after, reminder allowed.
Dependencies/Notes: Combine with limit and reset rules.

Requirement Description: Any change to edited data resets the reminder counter and overrides current cooldown.
Acceptance Criteria: After limit hit, making a change resets counter allowing up to 5 more reminders within policy; Change during active cooldown allows immediate reminder.
Dependencies/Notes: Clarify interplay with 24h global cap.

Requirement Description: Reminder counters and cooldown timers persist across app/service restarts.
Acceptance Criteria: Restarting does not clear counters or cooldown timers; Behavior documented and verified.
Dependencies/Notes: Requires durable store (e.g., Redis/DB).

Requirement Description: Track reminders per user/session concurrently without interference.
Acceptance Criteria: Two users can send reminders independently respecting their own limits/timers.
Dependencies/Notes: Per-user/supplier keying strategy.

Requirement Description: Individual supplier: when GST missing and prerequisites present, redirect to GST screen.
Acceptance Criteria: For scenarios 6.1–6.3: user lands on GST screen directly.
Dependencies/Notes: Define prerequisites: PAN present, Aadhaar present (MSME optional)

Requirement Description: Individual supplier: if PAN missing but GST step requested, redirect to PAN first, then GST.
Acceptance Criteria: For scenarios 8.1–8.3: user flows PAN → GST.
Dependencies/Notes: Enforce dependency: PAN before GST.

Requirement Description: Individual supplier: enforce PAN → GST → MSME navigation order.
Acceptance Criteria: For scenarios 9.1–9.3: user flows PAN → GST → MSME.
Dependencies/Notes: MSME captured after GST if applicable.

Requirement Description: Business supplier: when GST missing and PAN doesn't exist, redirect to PAN screen first, then GST screen.
Acceptance Criteria: For scenarios Business 5.1–5.3: user is redirected to PAN screen first (not GST screen directly).After PAN is added, user is then redirected to GST screen. Business suppliers follow PAN→GST flow similar to individual suppliers
Dependencies/Notes: Business suppliers require PAN before GST (same as individual suppliers). Update prerequisites: PAN must exist before GST redirect

Requirement Description: Business supplier: provide manual address entry path when auto-source unavailable; show Supplier vs Old PO address.
Acceptance Criteria: For scenarios Business 4.1–4.3: manual address entry enabled; Old PO address remains unchanged; new POs use new address.
Dependencies/Notes: Align with existing PO impact rules.
